message:
style(sync/tests): flatten validate_sync_paths error-variant assertions

Two tests asserting `BeadsError::Config(message)` with a nested
message check used the `match err { Variant(m) => ..., other =>
panic!() }` pattern. Rewrite each into a flat two-step form that
avoids the panic! arm and keeps the "unexpected error" message in
the same shape the rest of the test suite uses:

  assert!(matches!(&err, BeadsError::Config(_)), ...);
  let message = if let BeadsError::Config(m) = &err { m.as_str() }
                else { "" };
  assert!(message.contains("traversal" | "symlink"), ...);

No assertion coverage change; the two tests pin the same
invariants they did before. The `match + panic!` form was
copy-pasted across multiple tests; this is the start of converging
on one shape so a future variant rename doesn't need to touch the
`other => panic!(...)` arm of every test.

Cargo.lock intentionally not committed — still a local .cargo/
config.toml path-override artifact, not a semver change.
